events.d: NOT_APPLICABLE: acceptance assertions fully drive the CertSpec policy at this layer

NOT_APPLICABLE: the two activated acceptance tests fully drive RcgenCa::root (a single observable port operation translating the existing pure CertSpec::root); no unit decomposition needed. Adapter code uses real-infra integration tests, never mocked unit tests.
NOT_APPLICABLE: schema-evolution golden-bytes + discriminant triangulation + unknown-version probe tests fully drive the pure rkyv envelope; no internal decomposition to unit-test in isolation

NOT_APPLICABLE: L3 example-only ATs fully specify the codec behaviour (seal/open round-trip, K3 byte-scan, tampered-vs-wrong-KEK distinction); pure core Kek port surface (newtype getters + fixture impl) is exercised through the codec ATs end-to-end, no separate mocked unit layer needed

NOT_APPLICABLE: adapter/composition-root wiring is proven by the three L3 integration ATs driving the ca_boot port over real RcgenCa + LocalIntentStore + SystemdCredsKeyring + RootKeyAeadCodec; no separate mocked unit test would add behavioral coverage (Mandate 4 — adapters use real-infra integration tests, never mocks)

NOT_APPLICABLE: example-based AT fully drives the pure CertSpec::intermediate profile surface (role/is_ca/path_len/key_usages/key_usage_critical/subject) through the public port; no internal decomposition to unit-test in isolation. Mirrors 01-01/01-02/01-03. Single-URI-SAN PBT belongs to Slice 04.

NOT_APPLICABLE: single observable port operation (issue_intermediate) with no internal decomposition; the acceptance test exercises the full deterministic-issuance + chains-to-root surface through the Ca driving port. Same rationale as prior sim CA steps.
NOT_APPLICABLE: single observable port operation (issue_intermediate); adapter code is integration-tested via the three L3 ATs, not mocked — mirrors 01-03/03-01/03-02

NOT_APPLICABLE: single observable port operation (bootstrap_node_intermediate wiring issue_intermediate into node bootstrap); adapter/infra wiring exercised via the L3 integration AT with a real LocalIntentStore, never mocked unit tests; the AT fully drives the wiring and reaches GREEN without unit decomposition — mirrors 03-01/03-02/03-03

NOT_APPLICABLE: pure core policy; the two acceptance proptest properties (S-04-01 leaf-profile invariants, S-04-02 InvalidSan rejection) ARE the port-to-port coverage at the CertSpec::svid driving port — no separate decomposition layer to unit-test
NOT_APPLICABLE: sim-adapter method fully driven to GREEN through the Ca driving port by the two ATs (S-04-04/05); no separate unit decomposition needed (established pattern for 01-02/03-02)

PASS: activated S-04-07 (walking_skeleton) + S-04-08 (leaf profile) scaffolds; both failed for business-logic reason (issue_svid unimplemented) then driven GREEN. S-04-09 (bad-SAN reject) NOT activated: structurally unreachable through issue_svid(&SvidRequest) -- a valid single SpiffeId always projects to exactly one URI SAN, so CertSpec::svid cannot yield 0/>=2 at the adapter; satisfying it needs widening SvidRequest to a SAN set (overdrive-core, out of scope). Scaffold remains #[should_panic]; blocker surfaced for design decision.

NOT_APPLICABLE: RcgenCa is an adapter-host crypto adapter exercised exclusively by L3 integration ATs over real rcgen + real openssl verify (Mandate 4 -- adapters are integration-tested, never mocked unit tests). The two activated ATs (S-04-07 chain-verify, S-04-08 leaf profile via x509-parser) fully drive issue_svid through the Ca driving port to GREEN -- no separate decomposition layer to unit-test, matching the 01-03/03-03 pattern.

PASS: implemented RcgenCa::issue_svid (CA:FALSE single-URI-SAN leaf, digitalSignature critical, ~1h validity, Entropy serial, signed by cached node intermediate); refactored issue_intermediate to mint-once-and-cache so the leaf chains to the same intermediate. Full overdrive-host integration suite GREEN in Lima: 13/13 passed (incl. S-01/S-03 root+intermediate, S-04-07 openssl-verify chain exit 0, S-04-08 x509-parser leaf profile). clippy -D warnings clean. S-04-09 remains a #[should_panic] scaffold (structural blocker, see RED_ACCEPTANCE).

PASS: commit 18666841 (2 files: rcgen_ca.rs + rcgen_ca_chain_verify.rs). Lefthook gates passed (fmt, doctest, clippy, nextest-affected 1386 passed/0 failed). Delivers S-04-07 (walking skeleton) + S-04-08 (leaf profile). S-04-09 explicitly documented as BLOCKED in commit body -- bad-SAN reject unreachable through issue_svid(&SvidRequest) without widening SvidRequest (overdrive-core, out of scope); needs a design decision. Step 04-03 is a PARTIAL completion (2/3 scenarios); not logging the step as fully complete.

NOT_APPLICABLE: S-04-06 is an L3 cross-adapter equivalence guard fully driven through the Ca::issue_svid driving port over real crypto (x509-parser on real cert DER from both RcgenCa and SimCa); there is no separate decomposition layer to unit-test. The Option-A cleanup (rustdoc + dead CaError::InvalidSan removal + uniform error mapping) is non-behavioural refactor exercised by the existing host/sim issue_svid green tests, not new logic requiring a unit test.

NOT_APPLICABLE: golden-bytes + discriminant-offset triangulation + unknown-version probe ARE the L1 archive coverage (pure rkyv, no I/O); they exercise the public envelope/codec surface port-to-port. No GREEN decomposition needs additional unit tests — mirrors GREEN sibling 02-01 (root_ca_key.rs).

NOT_APPLICABLE: ATs fully drove GREEN — S-05-02 trust_bundle composition + S-05-01 faithful per-call serial observable need no decomposition unit tests (port-to-port through Ca trait); no complex GREEN to decompose
